data.title: Viktoria Mortensen launches Face Plasticity Rejuvenation Challenge as safer anti-aging alternative New York Oct. 3, 2025.
data.description: Based on tensegrity and osteopathic studies, a new program is providing an alternative to fillers, toxins, and surgeries for professionals and wellness clients. The initiative, launched in New York on Oct. 3, 2025, addresses the increasing demand for non-invasive procedures in the aesthetics sector worldwide.

data.title: Investors in Altimmune urged to contact Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP for losses between 08/10/23 - 06/25/25. Contact James Wilson for options.
data.description: Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson from Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is urging investors who experienced losses in Altimmune to reach out to him directly to explore potential courses of action. If you bought or obtained securities in Altimmune during the period from August 10, 2023, to June 25, 2025, and wish to discuss your options, please contact Mr. Wilson.

KALSHI PREDICTS 15-DAY SHUTDOWN Day 3 of the U.S. government shutdown. For the first time, forecasts hit 15 days — the longest outlook yet — as traders bet on a drawn-out standoff. Kalshi odds have climbed steadily since late September, with no settlement in sight.

data.title: Armor Health appoints JC Rogers as VP Business Development to drive national growth. MIAMI, Oct. 3, 2025.
data.description: In Miami on October 3, 2025, Armor Health, a prominent national integrated correctional healthcare provider, introduced JC Rogers as their new Vice President of Business Development. With this appointment, JC will spearhead Armor's sales strategy and growth initiatives. Armor Health, known for its quality services, can be found at ArmorHealthcare.com.

data.title: MEA AI-assisted Medical Imaging market to reach ~US$500M by 2032, growing at ~24% CAGR. GCC countries lead with over 60% spending. Sub Saharan Africa adopts low bandwidth DR/ultrasound AI.
data.description: A new report on the Middle East & Africa AI-assisted Medical Imaging Market predicts a significant growth trajectory, with the market expected to rise from approximately US$25 million in 2023 to around US$500 million by 2032, reflecting a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of about 24%. The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) leads the region, accounting for more than 60% of the total expenditure on enterprise AI suites, while Sub Saharan Africa is adopting low bandwidth AI solutions for digital radiography and ultrasound using operational expenditure models and teleradiology. The report offers strategic insights for stakeholders seeking opportunities in this rapidly expanding market, providing guidance on where to focus and how to achieve success on a country-specific basis.

data.title: Cannabidiol market forecasted to reach USD 27.57 billion by 2032 with CAGR of 16.5% from 2025. North America leads. Legalization boosts growth.
data.description: The Cannabidiol (CBD) Market is estimated to be valued at USD 9.46 billion in 2025, projected to reach USD 27.57 billion by 2032 with a 16.5% Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR). Hemp is the primary source for CBD production, expected to account for 58.6% market share in 2025. North America leads the market with over 40% share, while Asia Pacific is seen as a lucrative market. Increasing legalization and acceptance of CBD across the globe is fueling market growth, with nearly 50 countries legalizing cannabis for medical or recreational use. Despite restrictions and limited scientific evidence hindering market growth, escalating demand for natural wellness products is creating growth opportunities. Italy and Japan are examples of countries with strict regulations on CBD products.

data.title: Walmart's OnePay bank app to offer Bitcoin, Ether trading in 2021, aiming to be a US superapp like WeChat.
data.description: OnePay, a banking app supported by Walmart, is considering incorporating the trading and custody of Bitcoin and Ether later this year to enhance its services and transform into a US superapp similar to WeChat. This move aims to attract more users and increase engagement, marking a significant step in the company's expansion strategy.

data.title: Q-Gold closes $11.5M private placement financing with 76,666,667 Subscription Receipts at $0.15 each, including 15% over-allotment.
data.description: On October 3, 2025, Q-Gold Resources Ltd. (TSXV: QGR) announced the successful closing of its private placement offering of subscription receipts. The company issued a total of 76,666,667 subscription receipts at $0.15 each, resulting in aggregate gross proceeds of $11,500,000. This offering was facilitated through an agency agreement with BMO Capital Markets, with the agent exercising its over-allotment option in full.
